quote: Ramadan is the month, whose beginning is mercy, its middle, forgiveness, and its end, emancipation from the fire (of hell).
When the month of Ramadan begins, the gates of Paradise are opened and the gates of Hell are shut, and the devils are fettered.
Whoever does not give up false speech and evil actions during the fast, Allah is not in need of his leaving his food and drink.
Ramadan is the month of repentance.
Fasting is a shield with which a servant protects himself from the fire.
The month of Ramadan is the one in which the Quran was revealed—a guidance for mankind and clear proof for the guidance and the criterion (between right and wrong).
Ramadan is a time for reflection, renewal, and spiritual growth.
Ramadan is not just about fasting. It’s about purifying the soul and seeking closeness to Allah.
Ramadan teaches us self-discipline, patience, and empathy for those less fortunate.
Fasting in Ramadan is an exercise in self-control and a means to purify the body and mind.
The bond between family and community strengthens during Ramadan.
Ramadan reminds us to be grateful for the blessings we often take for granted.
During Ramadan, we focus on nourishing our souls rather than our bodies.
Ramadan is a time to seek forgiveness, mend broken relationships, and reconcile with others.
Ramadan is a time to seek knowledge and deepen our understanding of Islam.
Ramadan reminds us of the importance of compassion and helping those in need.
Ramadan is a time to reconnect with our inner selves and seek inner peace.
Fasting is not just about abstaining from food and drink; it is also about restraining oneself from evil thoughts and actions.
Have you ever wondered why we’re happy in Ramadan? Because we do what we were created to do.
O you who have believed, decreed upon you is fasting as it was decreed upon those before you that you may become righteous.
